perspectives.description: The boss is the person who insists on paying for the meal, believing it to be a gesture of goodwill and professionalism. Their stake lies in maintaining a positive relationship with the employee and demonstrating generosity.

The employee is the person who feels uncomfortable with others paying for their expenses and values independence. Their stake is in maintaining their personal principles while navigating the social dynamics of the dinner.

The future colleague is someone who may observe the interaction between the boss and the employee, potentially influencing their perception of workplace culture. Their stake is in understanding the dynamics of generosity and independence in professional relationships.
